title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Browser and N… · Type-System Trace: Relative Location Resolution
summary: A sign-in flow follows relative and cross-origin redirects while Fetch redirect modes, credentials, methods, and authorization scope differ. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts relative location resolution increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve Location references against the current request URL and validate the resulting destination each hop. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
